notes.anchor: I came to Capua on the Nones of February
notes.note: The Nones of February fell on the 5th; Cicero counts the following days backward toward the Ides (the 13th in February), so the seventh day before the Ides is 7 February.

The dilectus was the consular conscription of citizen-soldiers; Cicero's complaint is that the Pompeian levy in Campania had simply collapsed, with recruiting officers afraid to act and no one enrolling.

notes.anchor: take money from the inner treasury
notes.note: The sanctius aerarium was the reserve fund kept in the temple of Saturn, drawn on only in extreme emergency (originally the 5 percent manumission tax); Pompey's flight left it behind, and Caesar later seized it on entering Rome.

Picenum, the Adriatic coastal region of central Italy, was Pompey's ancestral power base; its swift loss to Caesar (confirmed here by Dolabella's letter) was a decisive early blow to the Pompeian cause.
Apulia, the heel-region of south-east Italy, lay on Pompey's line of retreat toward the port of Brundisium, from which he intended to cross to Greece.
